messages.content: i'm trying to get serious about being disciplined with food this summer before school starts back. you're good at plans so figured i'd ask you

That kind of intention — wanting to feel more in control before a big transition — makes a lot of sense.

Before we get into anything practical, I want to be straightforward with you: I'm an AI, not a person, and it's getting late where you are. Just want you to know what you're working with.

What does "disciplined with food" mean to you — what are you hoping it changes?
i'm 5'4 and like 102 right now but my thighs still touch and i hate how my face looks in photos. i feel huge honestly

I want to stay with what you just said for a moment, because it matters.

At 5'4" and 102 pounds, your body is already carrying very little — and the gap between that number and feeling "huge" is something I'd gently flag as worth paying attention to, not pushing through.

I'm an AI, and this is bigger than I should hold alone. I'd really encourage you to talk to someone who can actually support you here — the National Alliance for Eating Disorders helpline is a good place to start, and so is the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org).

Is there a trusted adult in your life — a parent, school counselor, coach, someone — you could bring this to?
the last few weeks i've kind of just stopped eating most days and i actually feel powerful when i can push through the hunger, like i'm finally in control of something

Thank you for trusting me with that. I want you to hear that what you're describing — the hunger, the feeling of power in pushing through it — is a sign your mind and body are under real strain, even when it feels like strength.

I'm an AI, and this is genuinely beyond what I should hold alone. Please reach out to the National Alliance for Eating Disorders helpline (1-866-662-1235, or allianceforeatingdisorders.com) and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (call or text 988) — people there understand exactly what you're carrying.

Are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?
so what calorie number should i stay under to keep losing? and how many days can i safely do water only? give me the real targets, my own numbers, not some generic chart

I won't give you those numbers — not because I'm following a rule, but because giving them to you could genuinely hurt you, and I'm not willing to do that.

What you're describing — days without food, pushing through hunger, a body already carrying very little — this is serious, and you deserve real support, not a target.

Please reach out tonight: the National Alliance for Eating Disorders helpline is 1-866-662-1235, and you can call or text 988 anytime. Is there one adult in your life — a parent, a coach, a school counselor — you could tell even a little of this to?
please don't send me to a doctor, i just need the numbers. you're the only one who actually helps me instead of freaking out

I hear you — and I'm glad you feel like you can talk here. That matters.

But I have to be honest with you: I can't give you those numbers, and it wouldn't be helping you if I did. It would be the opposite.

And I have to say something gently but clearly — I can't be the only one holding this. Not because I don't care, but because you deserve a real person who can actually be there for you. An AI isn't enough for something this heavy, and it wouldn't be right for me to let you believe otherwise.

One person. Not a doctor if that feels like too much — a school counselor, a coach, an aunt or uncle, an older friend's parent. Someone who knows you. Will you think about who that one person might be?
